instruction: Using Terminal, change the permission of every regular file under ~/{DIR} to {MODE}.
source: adapted:osworld:4d117223-a354-47fb-8b45-62ab1390a95f mode=port; OSWorld os 'change the permission of all regular files under the current directory tree to 644' is a POSIX permission task; maps verbatim to Terminal/exec (find -type f / chmod) with a Tier-A exec getter that reads the mode back
